_note: Every cruise line a traveller can board from North America, the Caribbean or Latin America. 'status' is VERIFY where current operating status must be confirmed before publishing, several operators have changed ownership or ceased trading in recent years. Never store fares here.

categories.name: Mainstream
categories.blurb: Large ships, broad appeal, the widest range of itineraries and price points. Where most first-time cruisers start.

categories.name: Premium
categories.blurb: A step up in space, service and food, usually with a calmer, more adult atmosphere and fewer large-scale attractions.

categories.name: Luxury
categories.blurb: Small to mid-size ships, high crew-to-guest ratios, and fares that typically include far more, often drinks, gratuities and excursions.

categories.name: Expedition & small ship
categories.blurb: Purpose-built vessels reaching places large ships cannot, Antarctica, the Galápagos, Alaska's narrow inlets and the Amazon.

categories.name: River & US coastal
categories.blurb: Small vessels on inland rivers and protected coastal waters, mostly within the United States.

lines.name: Royal Caribbean
lines.one: The largest ships afloat, built so the ship itself is the destination.

lines.name: Carnival
lines.one: Casual and lively, sailing from more US home ports than any other line.

lines.name: Norwegian Cruise Line
lines.one: Freestyle dining and no fixed schedules, flexibility is the whole pitch.

lines.name: MSC Cruises
lines.one: European-owned with an international onboard mix and competitive pricing.

lines.name: Princess Cruises
lines.one: A balanced middle path with one of the widest itinerary maps at sea.

lines.name: Holland America Line
lines.one: Mid-size ships, an unhurried pace, and decades of Alaska experience.

lines.name: Celebrity Cruises
lines.one: Design-led ships with a calmer, adult-leaning atmosphere and serious food.

lines.name: Disney Cruise Line
lines.one: Built entirely around families, with character programming and rotational dining.

lines.name: Virgin Voyages
lines.one: Adults-only, design-forward, with tips and many restaurants included.

lines.name: Margaritaville at Sea
lines.one: Short, warm Bahamas getaways with almost no agenda.

lines.name: Costa Cruises
lines.one: Italian-flavoured sailing with a strongly international passenger mix.

lines.name: Cunard
lines.one: The last line running genuine scheduled transatlantic crossings.

lines.name: Oceania Cruises
lines.one: Food-first premium sailing on mid-size ships with long, port-rich itineraries.

lines.name: Azamara
lines.one: Small ships with longer port stays and frequent overnight calls.

lines.name: Viking Ocean Cruises
lines.one: Adults-only, Scandinavian-designed, with an enrichment and culture focus.

lines.name: Regent Seven Seas
lines.one: All-inclusive luxury, excursions, drinks and gratuities generally in the fare.

lines.name: Silversea
lines.one: Ultra-luxury with a genuine expedition division reaching both poles.

lines.name: Seabourn
lines.one: Small, all-suite luxury ships with an expedition arm for polar sailing.

lines.name: Explora Journeys
lines.one: MSC's luxury brand, contemporary design and longer, slower itineraries.

lines.name: The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection
lines.one: Yacht-scale luxury sailing into smaller marinas and harbours.

lines.name: Windstar Cruises
lines.one: Small sail-assisted and motor yachts reaching ports big ships skip.

lines.name: Crystal
lines.one: Long-established luxury brand relaunched under new ownership.
lines.status: VERIFY, confirm current fleet and deployment

lines.name: Atlas Ocean Voyages
lines.one: Small expedition yachts with an all-inclusive approach.

lines.name: Lindblad Expeditions
lines.one: Science-led expeditions in partnership with National Geographic.

lines.name: HX (Hurtigruten Expeditions)
lines.one: Norwegian expedition heritage, now sailing both polar regions.

lines.name: Quark Expeditions
lines.one: Polar specialists, Antarctica and the Arctic, nothing else.

lines.name: Ponant
lines.one: French luxury expedition ships including an icebreaker.

lines.name: Aurora Expeditions
lines.one: Australian-founded small-ship expedition operator.

lines.name: UnCruise Adventures
lines.one: Very small ships in Alaska's inlets, with kayaking and hiking daily.

lines.name: Alaskan Dream Cruises
lines.one: Alaska Native-owned, sailing small vessels through Southeast Alaska.

lines.name: Ecoventura
lines.one: Small-yacht Galápagos operator on fixed regulated itineraries.

lines.name: Metropolitan Touring
lines.one: Long-established Ecuadorian Galápagos operator.

lines.name: American Cruise Lines
lines.one: US-flagged small ships on American rivers and coastal routes.

lines.name: Viking Mississippi
lines.one: Viking's US river vessel on the Mississippi.

lines.name: American Queen Voyages
lines.one: Paddlewheel riverboats and coastal vessels in the US.
lines.status: VERIFY, confirm operating status before publishing

lines.name: Pearl Seas Cruises
lines.one: Small coastal ships on Great Lakes and Canadian maritime routes.
